rationale: Stephen and Galak (2012) is discussed in depth in the conceptual background and comparison table as one of the key prior studies that jointly considers firm-generated and user-generated media. The citing paper uses it to motivate its core research gap, explicitly contrasting SG's microlending/blog context and nonsignificant FGC-to-UGC link with the current Twitter/movie setting. This citation informs the paper's conceptual setup (direct vs. indirect pathway) and positioning of contribution, not just general background.
relation_description: The citing paper builds on Stephen and Galak (2012) as a foundational prior study of media interplay and demand, then extends that line to Twitter-based firm-generated content for new movie releases. It reexamines the FGC-to-UGC-to-sales pathway in a new medium and context with additional source/type heterogeneity analyses.
